### 13. Write ADR-0063 Expo SDK 52->53 compatibility matrix (pre-flight)

- **id:** `adr-0055-expo-sdk-53-preflight` - **kind:** docs - **status:** needs-decision
- **Source:** `docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-22-mobile-expo-sdk-53.md`
- **Files:** `docs/adr/0063-expo-sdk-53-upgrade.md`, `docs/adr/README.md`

**Summary.** ADR-0055 is already occupied by `0055-openclaw-external-gateway.md`; the correct next free number is **0063** (confirmed by the `docs/adr/README.md` "Note on next ADR" line). The new file should be `docs/adr/0063-expo-sdk-53-upgrade.md`. Additionally, `apps/mobile/app.config.ts` already sets `newArchEnabled: true`, so the initiative's suggested mitigation of setting it to `false` is in conflict with the current state and requires an explicit owner decision before the upgrade PR. The patch drop path and TypeScript version implications are grounded in existing repo docs.

**Steps:**

1. 1. Confirm the ADR number: the task spec says 0055 but that number is taken. Use 0063 (the current next-free number per docs/adr/README.md). Ask @Skords-01 to confirm before writing (see openQuestions).
2. 2. Create docs/adr/0063-expo-sdk-53-upgrade.md with the content block in 'diffs' below. Status = Proposed.
3. 3. Update docs/adr/README.md: (a) add a row for 0063 in the 'Поточні ADR' table; (b) change the 'Note on next ADR' line from 0063 to 0064.
4. 4. The ADR references apps/mobile/package.json and patches/README.md — no edits to those files are needed for this docs-only PR.
5. 5. Open PR as PR-1 of the PR-22 rollout plan (standalone ADR, no code). Title: 'docs(adr): add ADR-0063 Expo SDK 52->53 pre-flight compatibility matrix'.

**Risks.** - ADR number conflict will cause `check-adr-graph.mjs` to fail if the file is committed as 0055 (duplicate). Using 0063 avoids this.\n- If SDK 53 GA ships before the ADR is merged, the TBC cells in the compatibility matrix will be stale immediately — reviewer must fill them from https://expo.dev/changelog before merging.\n- `newArchEnabled: true` already being ON means the initiative's 'safe default' mitigation is moot unless the upgrade PR explicitly reverts it.

**Verification.** Reviewer checks: (1) `node scripts/docs/check-adr-graph.mjs` passes (ADR graph integrity CI gate in docs-automation.yml). (2) `pnpm lint:patches` still passes — patches/README.md is not modified. (3) The ADR table row in docs/adr/README.md has a valid 4-digit number, kebab-case title, and Proposed status. (4) All package versions cited in the compatibility matrix are checked against https://expo.dev/changelog (SDK 53 release notes) by the reviewer before merge — the ADR is a living pre-flight doc and the matrix cells marked TBC must be filled once SDK 53 GA ships.

**Open questions.** 1. ADR number: the task spec says 0055 but that number is already `0055-openclaw-external-gateway.md`. Should the new file use 0063 (the current next-free number per README.md)? @Skords-01 must confirm before the file is created.\n\n2. New Architecture flag: `apps/mobile/app.config.ts` line 170 already has `newArchEnabled: true`. The initiative doc (pr-22-mobile-expo-sdk-53.md Risks table) suggests setting it to `false` as a mitigation. The ADR must record a conscious decision: keep New Arch ON (accepted risk, verify with Detox suite) OR explicitly set it to false in the upgrade PR. This changes the content of the Decision section.

<details><summary>Diffs / code blocks</summary>

````diff
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/adr/0063-expo-sdk-53-upgrade.md
@@ -0,0 +1,175 @@
+# ADR-0063: Expo SDK 52 → 53 pre-flight compatibility matrix
+
+- **Status:** Proposed
+- **Date:** 2026-06-06
+- **Deciders:** @Skords-01
+- **Supersedes:** —
+- **Related:**
+  - [`docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-22-mobile-expo-sdk-53.md`](../initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-22-mobile-expo-sdk-53.md)
+  - [`ADR-0050`](./0050-typescript-major-version-policy.md) — TypeScript version policy (mobile TS pin)
+  - [`ADR-0052`](./0052-mobile-strategy-capacitor-primary.md) — Capacitor primary; Expo parallel track
+  - [`ADR-0019`](./0019-push-notifications.md) — Push notifications (expo-notifications dependency)
+  - [`patches/README.md`](../../patches/README.md) — `@expo__cli@0.22.28.patch` drop condition
+
+---
+
+## Context and Problem Statement
+
+`apps/mobile` is pinned to **Expo SDK 52** (`expo@~52.0.0`, `react-native@0.76.9`).
+Expo SDK 53 GA is expected ~June 2026 and ships with **React Native 0.77**.
+
+Per the lag-policy in PR-22 (N+1 rule), SDK 53 should be adopted within ~6 months of GA to
+avoid EAS Build image deprecation, Expo Go SDK-52 EOL, and exclusion from RN 0.77+ security
+patches.
+
+This ADR is the **pre-flight record only** (PR-22 PR-1 slice). The actual code bump
+(PR-22 PR-2) is out of scope and must not start until this ADR is merged and the open
+questions below are resolved.
+
+## Considered Options
+
+1. **Upgrade to SDK 53 at GA + 2-week stability window** — adopt once community reports
+   stabilise; gives time for native dep authors to publish SDK-53-compatible releases.
+2. **Stay on SDK 52 until forced** — delay until EAS Build image deprecation forces the
+   hand; maximises compatibility knowledge but risks cliff-edge migration under time pressure.
+3. **Skip SDK 53, target SDK 54** — unlikely to be viable; skipping a major Expo release is
+   not supported by EAS or Expo Go.
+
+## Decision
+
+Adopt **Option 1**: upgrade to Expo SDK 53 after GA + 2-week community stability window
+(target: Q3 2026). The upgrade is gated on the compatibility matrix below reaching full
+green status (no `TBC` or `BLOCKED` cells) before PR-22 PR-2 is opened.
+
+### New Architecture flag
+
+`apps/mobile/app.config.ts` currently has `newArchEnabled: true` (already opted in during
+a prior PR). SDK 53 makes New Architecture the default for new projects.
+
+**Decision on `newArchEnabled`**: <!-- DECISION NEEDED — see openQuestions -->
+Keep `newArchEnabled: true` and validate via the full Detox suite in PR-22 PR-2. If Detox
+or soak testing reveals a regression, revert to `newArchEnabled: false` in that same PR
+with a follow-up ADR for the New Architecture opt-in.
+
+---
+
+## Compatibility Matrix
+
+> Legend: ✅ ready | ⚠️ minor breaking change (patch documented) | ❌ blocked | TBC (check at SDK 53 GA)
+>
+> **All version cells marked TBC must be verified against
+> https://expo.dev/changelog and the Expo SDK 53 release notes before PR-22 PR-2 is opened.**
+
+### Expo / React Native core
+
+| Package | SDK 52 version | SDK 53 min version | Status | Notes |
+|---------|---------------|-------------------|--------|-------|
+| `expo` | `~52.0.0` | `~53.0.0` | TBC | Run `npx expo install expo@~53.0.0` |
+| `react-native` | `0.76.9` | `0.77.x` | TBC | See RN 0.76→0.77 breaking changes below |
+| `react` | `18.3.1` | `18.3.x` | TBC | Likely unchanged |
+| `expo-router` | `~4.0.21` | TBC | TBC | Check for v4.x point release or v5 |
+| `jest-expo` | `~52.0.6` | TBC | TBC | Must match SDK version exactly |
+| `@expo/metro-runtime` | `~4.0.1` | TBC | TBC | |
+
+### Expo native modules (used by apps/mobile)
+
+| Package | SDK 52 version | SDK 53 min version | Status | Notes |
+|---------|---------------|-------------------|--------|-------|
+| `expo-notifications` | `~0.29.14` | TBC | TBC | APNs / FCM registration (ADR-0019) |
+| `expo-camera` | `~16.0.0` | TBC | TBC | Barcode scan |
+| `expo-constants` | `~17.0.8` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-crypto` | `~14.0.2` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-dev-client` | `~5.0.20` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-document-picker` | `~13.0.3` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-file-system` | `~18.0.12` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-haptics` | `~14.0.1` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-image-manipulator` | `~13.0.6` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-image-picker` | `~16.0.6` | TBC | TBC | Requires permissions re-check on SDK 53 |
+| `expo-keep-awake` | `~14.0.1` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-linear-gradient` | `~14.0.2` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-linking` | `~7.0.5` | TBC | TBC | Deep link scheme unchanged |
+| `expo-network` | `~7.0.5` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-secure-store` | `~14.0.1` | TBC | TBC | Used for Better Auth token storage |
+| `expo-sharing` | `~13.0.1` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-speech` | `~13.0.1` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-speech-recognition` | `~1.1.1` | TBC | TBC | Phase 8 voice; check Android STT API |
+| `expo-splash-screen` | `~0.29.24` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-sqlite` | `~15.1.4` | TBC | TBC | Local-first storage (ADR-0011) |
+| `expo-status-bar` | `~2.0.1` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `expo-web-browser` | `~14.0.2` | TBC | TBC | |
+
+### Community / third-party native deps
+
+| Package | SDK 52 version | SDK 53 min version | Status | Notes |
+|---------|---------------|-------------------|--------|-------|
+| `@better-auth/expo` | `^1.6.14` | TBC | TBC | Session cookie bridge |
+| `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` | `1.23.1` | TBC | TBC | Run `npx expo install --fix` |
+| `@react-native-community/netinfo` | `11.4.1` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `@sentry/react-native` | `~6.10.0` | TBC | TBC | Sentry native bridge |
+| `@shopify/flash-list` | `1.7.3` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `nativewind` | `^4.1.23` | TBC | TBC | Tailwind on RN; check RN 0.77 compat |
+| `react-native-gesture-handler` | `~2.20.2` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `react-native-mmkv` | `~3.2.0` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `react-native-reanimated` | `~3.16.1` | TBC | TBC | Known RN 0.77 breaking changes in 3.x |
+| `react-native-safe-area-context` | `4.12.0` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `react-native-screens` | `~4.4.0` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `react-native-svg` | `15.8.0` | TBC | TBC | |
+| `react-native-web` | `~0.19.13` | TBC | TBC | Web bundler target |
+| `victory-native` | `^36.9.2` | TBC | TBC | Charts |
+
+---
+
+## React Native 0.76 → 0.77 Breaking-Change Checklist
+
+To be verified against https://github.com/facebook/react-native/releases/tag/v0.77.0
+before PR-22 PR-2 is opened.
+
+- [ ] **New Architecture default-on**: RN 0.77 enables Fabric + TurboModules by default for
+  new projects. `apps/mobile` already has `newArchEnabled: true` (see Decision above).
+  Validate that every native module in the matrix above has a Fabric-compatible release.
+- [ ] **Metro config API changes**: check `apps/mobile/metro.config.js` against the
+  RN 0.77 Metro version (expected ~0.81.x). Custom resolvers or serializers may need updates.
+- [ ] **Hermes**: confirm Hermes is the default JS engine in RN 0.77 (was already default in
+  0.76); no action expected unless there is a Hermes version bump.
+- [ ] **Android Gradle Plugin**: RN 0.77 typically bumps the required AGP version.
+  Check `apps/mobile/android/gradle.properties` for the `android.useAndroidX` and AGP
+  version after `npx expo install --fix`.
+- [ ] **iOS deployment target**: RN 0.77 may raise the minimum iOS version. Confirm against
+  the current `ios.deploymentTarget` in `app.config.ts` / generated `Podfile`.
+- [ ] **`StyleSheet.compose` / deprecated APIs**: audit for any RN API removals listed in the
+  0.77 release notes that are used in `apps/mobile/src/` or `apps/mobile/app/`.
+- [ ] **`react-native-reanimated` 3.x + RN 0.77**: reanimated has historically had
+  breaking changes on each RN major; confirm the minimum reanimated version for RN 0.77.
+- [ ] **expo-router**: confirm whether SDK 53 ships expo-router v4.x or v5; check for
+  breaking file-based routing API changes.
+
+---
+
+## `@expo__cli@0.22.28.patch` — Rebase / Drop Plan
+
+The patch (`patches/@expo__cli@0.22.28.patch`) wraps `require("tar")` in `npm.js` to fix
+a `tar` v7 ESM/CJS interop crash in `expo prebuild`. Its documented drop condition
+(from `patches/README.md`):
+
+> Drop when: Expo CLI ≥ 0.23.x lands the upstream-side fix (or downgrade to `tar` v6
+> transitively). PR-22 (Expo SDK 53 upgrade) will drop this patch automatically —
+> verify after Expo SDK 53 GA.
+
+**Plan for PR-22 PR-2:**
+
+1. After `npx expo install expo@~53.0.0`, run `pnpm install`. If `pnpm install` fails with
+   a patch-apply error, it means `@expo/cli` was bumped past `0.22.28` and the patch no
+   longer applies — this is the drop signal.
+2. Verify that `expo prebuild` succeeds without the patch by testing against the new
+   `@expo/cli` version.
+3. If upstream has landed the fix: **drop** the patch file and the
+   `pnpm.patchedDependencies` entry, remove the row from `patches/README.md`.
+4. If upstream has NOT yet landed the fix: **rebase** the patch to match the new
+   `@expo/cli` version (same one-liner change; re-run `pnpm patch @expo/cli@<new-version>`).
+5. Either outcome must be documented in the PR-22 PR-2 description.
+
+---
+
+## Consequences
+
+### Positive
+
+- Stays within the N+1 lag-policy; avoids forced EAS image deprecation cliff.
+- RN 0.77 security patches become available.
+- SDK 53 TypeScript constraint may allow `apps/mobile` to move from TS 5.9.x to TS 6.x
+  (per ADR-0050 §3 — confirm with Expo SDK 53 release notes).
+- `@expo__cli` patch is likely dropped, reducing maintenance surface.
+
+### Negative
+
+- Full Detox iOS + Android suite + 7-day soak required (3–5 days effort, per PR-22 spec).
+- If any critical native dep is not ready at GA + 2 weeks, the upgrade must be delayed.
+
+### Neutral
+
+- `eas.json` build profiles contain no SDK version hardcoding; no changes expected there.
+- `app.config.ts` `runtimeVersion: { policy: "sdkVersion" }` automatically tracks the
+  new SDK version after the bump.
+
+## Compliance
+
+- PR-22 PR-2 **must not be opened** until all TBC cells in the compatibility matrix above
+  are resolved (green or blocked-with-mitigation) by the mobile engineer owning the upgrade.
+- The `check-adr-graph.mjs` CI gate will validate this ADR is listed in
+  `docs/adr/README.md` and has a valid `Status:` field.
+- `patches/README.md` must be updated in the same PR as the patch drop/rebase (existing
+  `pnpm lint:patches` CI gate enforces this).
+
+## Links
+
+- [`docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-22-mobile-expo-sdk-53.md`](../initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-22-mobile-expo-sdk-53.md) — source initiative
+- [`patches/README.md`](../../patches/README.md) — `@expo__cli@0.22.28.patch` drop condition
+- [`docs/adr/0050-typescript-major-version-policy.md`](./0050-typescript-major-version-policy.md) — TS version policy (mobile TS 5.9 pin)
+- [`docs/adr/0052-mobile-strategy-capacitor-primary.md`](./0052-mobile-strategy-capacitor-primary.md) — Expo parallel track context
+- [Expo SDK release changelog](https://expo.dev/changelog)
````

### 14. Write docs/security/internal-api-keys.md — dual-key rotation runbook

- **id:** `internal-api-key-rotation-runbook` - **kind:** docs - **status:** needs-decision
- **Source:** `docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-27-internal-api-key-rotation.md`
- **Files:** `E:\.claude\Sergeant\docs\security\internal-api-keys.md`, `E:\.claude\Sergeant\docs\security\secret-ownership-register.md`, `E:\.claude\Sergeant\docs\initiatives\stack-pulse-2026-05\pr-27-internal-api-key-rotation.md`, `E:\.claude\Sergeant\apps\server\src\routes\internal\index.ts`, `E:\.claude\Sergeant\apps\server\src\env\env.ts`, `E:\.claude\Sergeant\docs\security\api-internal-hmac.md`

**Summary.** docs/security/internal-api-keys.md does not yet exist. The dual-key rotation design is fully specified in docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-27-internal-api-key-rotation.md but the runbook (a PR-27 DoD item) is missing. This plan produces the exact file content, grounded in the actual code (routes/internal/index.ts, env.ts, the four consumer routes, the HMAC playbook, and the secret-ownership register). One decision is needed before the doc can be considered final: the CLI invocation syntax for /internal-key commands is not yet implemented, so the runbook must either describe the planned commands as pre-implementation stubs or omit the CLI section until the code lands.

**Steps:**

1. Create the directory: docs/security/ already exists (confirmed by ls).
2. Create docs/security/internal-api-keys.md with the exact content in the diffs block below.
3. Add an entry for INTERNAL_API_KEY in docs/security/secret-ownership-register.md (see diffs block for the exact table row to append).
4. No code changes — this is doc-only per the task scope; the migration + middleware is a separate task.

**Risks.** 1. The runbook documents the planned CLI commands (/internal-key list/create/revoke/audit) before the code exists — if the command signatures change during implementation, the runbook will need updating in the same PR. 2. The four consumer names (mono-webhook, openclaw-callback, n8n-alerts, admin-cli) are derived from the initiative doc, not yet from actual DB rows. If the implementation uses different names, the runbook table and TTL policy table need to be updated. 3. The 'bootstrap' key TTL (30 days) and all-scopes wildcard are drawn from the initiative doc — they should be confirmed against the actual migration SQL when it's written.

**Verification.** A reviewer confirms correctness by: (1) `grep -r 'internal-api-keys' docs/` returns the new file and any cross-ref link; (2) the lifecycle Status header is present and reads 'Scaffolded' (since the DB table and CLI are not yet merged, the runbook describes planned behaviour); (3) the four consumer names match exactly what is in routes/internal/index.ts (mono, openclaw, alerts, admin-cli); (4) the key-hash algorithm (bcrypt) and overlap window (24 h) match the initiative doc; (5) `pnpm check:tech-debt-freshness` (or equivalent) does not error on the new file.

**Open questions.** 1. The /internal-key CLI commands (list / create / revoke / audit) are in the PR-27 scope but not yet implemented in tools/openclaw. Should the runbook document them as 'planned — not yet live' with a note to update after the code merges, OR should the CLI section be omitted entirely and added in the same PR that ships the commands? This affects whether the Status badge should be 'Scaffolded' or 'Active'.\n2. Should the runbook be written in English (matching api-internal-hmac.md style) or Ukrainian (matching secret-rotation.md style)? Both conventions exist in docs/security/. The diff below uses English to match the HMAC playbook (which is the closest analogue), but the team may prefer Ukrainian.

=== NEW FILE: E:\.claude\Sergeant\docs\security\internal-api-keys.md ===

```diff
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/security/internal-api-keys.md
@@ -0,0 +1,222 @@
+# Internal API Key — rotation, audit, and revocation runbook
+
+> **Last validated:** 2026-06-06 by @Skords-01. **Next review:** 2026-09-06.
+> **Status:** Scaffolded — describes the dual-key DB-backed design from PR-27. The `internal_api_keys` table, `requireInternalApiKey` middleware, and `/internal-key` CLI commands are **not yet merged**. Until PR-27 lands, the current auth mechanism is the single `INTERNAL_API_KEY` env-var bearer described in [§ Legacy env-var](#legacy-env-var-pre-pr-27).
+> **Owner:** ops + server.
+> **Related:** [`api-internal-hmac.md`](../docs/security/api-internal-hmac.md), [`secret-ownership-register.md`](../docs/security/secret-ownership-register.md), [`docs/playbooks/rotate-secrets.md`](../docs/playbooks/rotate-secrets.md), [`docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-27-internal-api-key-rotation.md`](../docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-27-internal-api-key-rotation.md).
+
+---
+
+## Overview
+
+`INTERNAL_API_KEY` guards all machine-to-machine endpoints under `/api/internal/*`.
+Four consumers exist:
+
+| Consumer | Key name (post-PR-27) | Route prefix | Caller |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| Monobank webhook rotate | `mono-webhook` | `/api/internal/mono/*` | n8n cron |
+| OpenClaw bot callbacks | `openclaw-callback` | `/api/internal/openclaw/*` | `tools/openclaw` Railway service |
+| Alerts router | `n8n-alerts` | `/api/internal/alerts/*` | n8n WF-03/15/18/22/103–106 |
+| Admin CLI (OpenClaw `/internal-key` commands) | `admin-cli` | any | `tools/openclaw` ops role |
+
+Auth is layered:
+1. **Bearer token** — `Authorization: Bearer <key>` checked via constant-time compare.
+2. **HMAC-SHA256** — optional `X-Signature` + `X-Timestamp` (see [`api-internal-hmac.md`](../docs/security/api-internal-hmac.md)).
+3. **IP allowlist** — loopback + Railway internal CIDR (`requireInternalIp` middleware, M14).
+
+---
+
+## Legacy env-var (pre-PR-27)
+
+Until the `internal_api_keys` DB table is merged, the single shared secret lives in:
+
+```
+Railway → sergeant-server → Variables → INTERNAL_API_KEY
+```
+
+All four consumers use this single value. Rotation requires coordinating all four
+simultaneously (see [§ Emergency rotation — legacy](#emergency-rotation--legacy-env-var-only)).
+
+---
+
+## Post-PR-27: dual-key DB-backed rotation
+
+After PR-27 merges, each consumer has its own named row in `internal_api_keys`:
+
+```sql
+-- schema summary (full DDL: apps/server/src/migrations/NNN_internal_api_keys.sql)
+id          UUID PRIMARY KEY
+key_hash    TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE   -- bcrypt hash of the raw key
+name        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE   -- 'mono-webhook' | 'openclaw-callback' | 'n8n-alerts' | 'admin-cli'
+scopes      TEXT[]                 -- ['mono.write'] etc.
+expires_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL   -- TTL is mandatory
+created_by  TEXT NOT NULL
+last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ
+revoked_at  TIMESTAMPTZ
+```
+
+The bootstrap env-var `INTERNAL_API_KEY` seeds a row `name='bootstrap'` with a 30-day
+expiry on startup. After all consumers are migrated, the env-var is dropped (PR-27 PR-3).
+
+### Dual-key overlap window
+
+During rotation, two rows for the same `name` may coexist — both are valid for up to
+24 hours. The middleware accepts **any non-revoked, non-expired row** matching the
+presented hash. After consumer migration, revoke the old row.
+
+```
+t=0h   Generate new key for 'mono-webhook'  → row A (old, active) + row B (new, active)
+t=0–24h  Both keys valid; update n8n env to new key; redeploy
+t=24h  Confirm n8n uses new key (check last_used_at on row B)
+t=24h  Revoke row A: /internal-key revoke mono-webhook --id <old-uuid>
+```
+
+---
+
+## Planned rotation: routine (post-PR-27)
+
+Run before key expiry (default TTL: 90 days). Use the `/internal-key` commands
+in `tools/openclaw` (role: `ops`):
+
+### 1. Check current key status
+
+```
+/internal-key list
+```
+
+Returns: `name`, `expires_at` (Europe/Kyiv tz), `last_used_at`, `id`.
+Flag any key within 14 days of expiry.
+
+### 2. Generate a new key
+
+```
+/internal-key create <name> <ttl-days> <scopes>
+```
+
+Example:
+```
+/internal-key create mono-webhook 90 mono.write
+```
+
+The raw key is returned **once** — copy it immediately. The CLI does not store it.
+Both old and new keys are now valid.
+
+### 3. Update the consumer
+
+| Consumer | Where to update |
+|---|---|
+| `mono-webhook` | n8n WF-03 HTTP-Request node header `Authorization` → new key; redeploy |
+| `openclaw-callback` | Railway `tools/openclaw` service → Variables → `INTERNAL_API_KEY` → new key; redeploy |
+| `n8n-alerts` | n8n WF-15/18/22/103–106 → same as mono-webhook pattern; update `ops/n8n-workflows/03-sentry-alert-routing.json` and `ops/n8n-workflows/18-nightly-security-audit.json` |
+| `admin-cli` | Same Railway service as openclaw-callback |
+
+### 4. Verify new key is in use
+
+Wait at least one natural trigger of the consumer (cron fire, next DM to OpenClaw, etc.), then:
+
+```
+/internal-key audit <name> --since 1h
+```
+
+Confirm `last_used_at` has advanced on the new row. If not, the consumer is still using
+the old key — check Railway deploy logs.
+
+### 5. Revoke old key
+
+```
+/internal-key revoke <name> --id <old-uuid>
+```
+
+Or revoke by name (revokes oldest non-expired row for that name):
+
+```
+/internal-key revoke <name>
+```
+
+Revocation sets `revoked_at = NOW()`. The middleware rejects the old key immediately
+(LRU cache TTL: 5 min max — wait 5 min before declaring the old key dead in logs).
+
+---
+
+## Emergency rotation — suspected leak (post-PR-27)
+
+**Severity: P0. Time-box: < 30 min.**
+
+1. **Revoke immediately** — do not wait for consumer migration:
+   ```
+   /internal-key revoke <name>
+   ```
+   This sets `revoked_at` and invalidates the key within the 5-min LRU TTL.
+   Affected consumers will receive `401 Unauthorized` until step 3 is complete.
+
+2. **Generate replacement key** (see step 2 in routine rotation above).
+
+3. **Update consumers** in parallel if possible (see table in §3 above).
+   For `openclaw-callback` + `admin-cli` — same Railway service, single env-var update.
+
+4. **Verify** — `/internal-key audit <name> --since 30m` shows requests succeeding
+   on the new row.
+
+5. **Post-mortem** — open a GitHub issue referencing this runbook and the
+   `tg_internal_key_usage` audit log slice. Tag `internal_key_name` was added to
+   every Sentry transaction at the `requireInternalApiKey` middleware layer — use it
+   to bound the blast radius (which routes were called with the leaked key and when).
+
+---
+
+## Emergency rotation — legacy env-var only (pre-PR-27)
+
+Until the DB migration lands, all four consumers share one env-var. Rotation breaks
+all of them simultaneously — coordinate redeploys.
+
+1. Generate new secret:
+   ```
+   openssl rand -hex 32
+   ```
+
+2. Update Railway `sergeant-server` env `INTERNAL_API_KEY` → new value.
+   Railway triggers a redeploy automatically.
+
+3. While server is deploying, update n8n credentials:
+   - n8n UI → Credentials → find the HTTP credential used by WF-03/15/18/22 → update bearer value.
+   - Or: update directly in `ops/n8n-workflows/*.json` and re-import.
+
+4. Update `tools/openclaw` Railway service env `INTERNAL_API_KEY` → same new value → redeploy.
+
+5. Confirm: trigger `/openclaw status` in Telegram. If `200 OK` is returned, the OpenClaw
+   → server link is healthy.
+
+6. Confirm n8n: manually trigger WF-03 test run → check execution log for `200`.
+
+7. Update `docs/security/secret-rotation.md` with the rotation date.
+
+---
+
+## Audit
+
+### Post-PR-27 (DB-backed)
+
+```
+/internal-key audit <name> --since <ISO-date>
+```
+
+Returns: call counts, last caller IP (via Sentry tag), last `last_used_at`.
+Each request sets `last_used_at` (throttled: update only if `now - last_used_at > 60s`
+to limit write amplification).
+Sentry tag `internal_key_name` is added on every request through the middleware —
+use Sentry Issues → tag filter for forensic analysis.
+
+### Pre-PR-27 (env-var, no audit trail)
+
+No per-key usage tracking exists. Use Railway log search:
+```
+grep '"path":"/api/internal/' logs | jq '{ts:.time,path:.path,status:.statusCode}'
+```
+Or Grafana: `http_request_duration_ms_count{route=~"/api/internal/.*"}`.
+
+---
+
+## TTL and expiry policy
+
+| Key name | Default TTL | Scopes |
+|---|---|---|
+| `mono-webhook` | 90 days | `mono.write` |
+| `openclaw-callback` | 90 days | `openclaw.read`, `openclaw.write` |
+| `n8n-alerts` | 90 days | `alerts.read`, `alerts.write` |
+| `admin-cli` | 30 days | `internal.admin` |
+| `bootstrap` (env-var seed) | 30 days | `*` (all scopes) |
+
+Keys expire hard at `expires_at` — middleware returns `401` with `code: KEY_EXPIRED`.
+Rotate before expiry; the nightly security audit workflow (WF-18) alerts on keys
+within 14 days of expiry.
+
+---
+
+## See also
+
+- Initiative doc: [`docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-27-internal-api-key-rotation.md`](../docs/initiatives/stack-pulse-2026-05/pr-27-internal-api-key-rotation.md)
+- HMAC signing layer: [`docs/security/api-internal-hmac.md`](../docs/security/api-internal-hmac.md)
+- Bearer guard implementation: [`apps/server/src/routes/internal/index.ts`](../apps/server/src/routes/internal/index.ts)
+- Secret ownership register: [`docs/security/secret-ownership-register.md`](../docs/security/secret-ownership-register.md)
+- Rotate-secrets playbook: [`docs/playbooks/rotate-secrets.md`](../docs/playbooks/rotate-secrets.md)
```

### 15. Reconcile duplicate Last-validated bulk-bump dates across core READMEs

- **id:** `adr-graph-dedupe-last-validated-dates` - **kind:** docs - **status:** plan-ready
- **Source:** `docs/audits/2026-05-02-doc-hygiene-audit.md`
- **Files:** `E:\.claude\Sergeant\README.md`

**Summary.** The audit finding (#1.3) identified six files sharing a bulk-bumped `Last validated: 2026-05-02` date. Investigation shows that most of those files have already been updated to honest recent dates via subsequent commits. Only `README.md` still carries a stale header (`2026-05-13`) while its last substantive content edit landed on 2026-05-19 (commit `b8a709acb`, "3-agent drift hunt"). The other five files all have accurate, recent headers and require no changes.

**Steps:**

1. Read E:\.claude\Sergeant\README.md line 9 to confirm the current header text before editing.
2. Edit README.md line 9: change `Last validated: 2026-05-13 by @Skords-01 / Devin. **Next review:** 2026-08-11.` to `Last validated: 2026-05-19 by @Skords-01. **Next review:** 2026-08-17.` (90 days from 2026-05-19 = 2026-08-17; drop 'Devin' since the 2026-05-19 commit was a solo drift-hunt agent pass).
3. Open a PR titled `docs(root): update README.md freshness header to 2026-05-19` targeting main with a one-line body: 'Closes audit finding 1.3 — sets Last validated to the actual last-content-edit commit b8a709acb (2026-05-19) instead of the stale bulk-bump date 2026-05-13.'

**Risks.** Minimal. Only one line in one file changes. The 2026-06-02 Prettier-only commit (`3907703ef`) technically touched README.md but made zero semantic changes (table column spacing only), so attributing the validation date to 2026-05-19 is the honest choice. If policy requires counting any commit as a validation touch, the date could be set to 2026-06-02 instead — but that would misrepresent a formatting-only pass as a content review.

**Verification.** The `docs-freshness.yml` CI workflow runs `pnpm check:freshness` which validates that every tracked file's `Last validated` date is within the allowed staleness window. After the edit, the workflow should pass. A reviewer can also confirm manually: `git log -1 --format='%ai' -- README.md` shows the most recent content-changing commit was 2026-05-19 (`b8a709acb`), and the updated header matches that date. No code paths are affected — this is a doc-only header change.

**Open questions.** None required for implementation. Optional: decide whether auto-formatting commits (Prettier-only) should advance the `Last validated` date — if yes, change the target date from 2026-05-19 to 2026-06-02 and add `by @claude`.

### 17. Tighten check-bundle-size.mjs main-chunk budget per 0017 follow-up

- **id:** `0017-bundle-size-budget-followup` - **kind:** code - **status:** needs-decision
- **Source:** `docs/initiatives/follow-ups.md`
- **Files:** `E:\.claude\Sergeant\scripts\check-bundle-size.mjs`, `E:\.claude\Sergeant\docs\initiatives\0017-hub-tabs-mount-perf.md`, `E:\.claude\Sergeant\docs\initiatives\follow-ups.md`

**Summary.** Initiative 0017 shipped Sprint 1 (14 per-section lazy chunks) and Sprint 2 (5 per-card lazy chunks), moving significant JS off the main `index-` entry point. The follow-up calls for tightening the `index-` budget in `scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs` from 80 KB to ~30 KB (target: -50 KB) and adding a per-chunk cap for the new section/card chunks. The exact post-split gzip sizes can only be confirmed by running `pnpm build:web` in CI against the merged Sprint 1/2 state, which is the only genuine blocker. The plan below prepares the script edit and carry-over text update with a placeholder that CI must fill in and a rationale comment documenting the split rationale.

**Steps:**

1. 1. Confirm in CI: after merging Sprint 1/2 changes, trigger `pnpm build:web` on main and capture the gzipped `index-*.js` size from `apps/web/dist/assets/`. Record the exact KB number — this is the value that replaces the placeholder `<MEASURED>` in the diff below.
2. 2. Decide the new `index-` budget ceiling (see openQuestions). The initiative targets -50 KB (i.e. 30 KB), but the actual measured value after the split may land between 25-40 KB. Choose a number that is ≥ measured-value and reflects real post-split state, not an aspirational future reduction.
3. 3. Edit `scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs` BUDGETS object: lower `index-` maxSize from 80 to the confirmed value; add per-chunk entries for the new section/card chunks; update the block comment above BUDGETS. See the unified diff below.
4. 4. Edit `docs/initiatives/0017-hub-tabs-mount-perf.md` carry-over item: replace the open checkbox for the bundle-size item with a checked-off line recording the measured value and the new budget. See the doc block below.
5. 5. Re-generate `docs/initiatives/follow-ups.md` (auto-generated): run `pnpm docs:gen-initiative-followups` to remove the now-closed carry-over item from the index. Commit both the initiative file and the regenerated follow-ups.md in the same PR.
6. 6. Verify CI gate `Initiative follow-ups (in sync)` passes (it checks that the checked-in follow-ups.md equals the generated output).
7. 7. Run `node --test scripts/__tests__/check-bundle-size.test.mjs` to confirm the shape tests still pass (maxSize > 0, every prefix has a fixture match). No new fixture is needed for the new section/card entries because those chunks fall through to `_total` tracking, not individual BUDGETS keys — unless the reviewer decides to add named prefix entries for each section chunk (see openQuestions).

**Risks.** The `<MEASURED>` placeholder must not be committed as-is — it would make the script fail to parse. The applying engineer must substitute a real integer before the file is merged. The test suite (`check-bundle-size.test.mjs`) validates `maxSize > 0` and would catch a non-numeric value at test time, not at parse time. If per-section BUDGETS entries are added without matching fixtures in the test file, the regression guard test ('every non-catch-all BUDGETS prefix matches at least one real fixture') will fail in CI — this is intentional protection but must be planned for.

**Verification.** CI confirms correctness via: (1) `node --test scripts/__tests__/check-bundle-size.test.mjs` — shape tests pass, maxSize is a positive number; (2) `pnpm check` (format:check + lint + typecheck + test + build) runs clean; (3) `node scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs` exits 0 against the built `dist/assets` output — meaning the new tighter budget is not violated by the actual build; (4) the `Initiative follow-ups (in sync)` CI gate passes after `pnpm docs:gen-initiative-followups` is run and both `0017-hub-tabs-mount-perf.md` + `follow-ups.md` are committed in the same PR.

**Open questions.** 1. REQUIRED — Measured index- gzip size: What does `dist/assets/index-*.js` weigh (gzipped) after `pnpm build:web` on the current main branch (Sprint 1+2 merged)? This single number replaces every `<MEASURED>` placeholder. The initiative expects roughly 25-30 KB; if it is higher than 30 KB, the budget should be set at measured+5 KB headroom (not the aspirational 30 KB), and a separate follow-up should track the remaining reduction. 2. OPTIONAL — Per-section/card chunk entries in BUDGETS: The initiative Finalize note says 'add separate per-chunk gate (≤ 25 KB gzip each section/card chunk)'. Should individual BUDGETS prefix entries be added for each section chunk (e.g. `DashboardSection-`, `FinykSection-`, `ExpensesCard-`, etc.) in this same PR? If yes, their fixture names must also be added to `REAL_CHUNK_FIXTURES` in the test file. If no, the existing `_total` gate is the only guard and section/card chunks fall into 'uncategorized' in the CLI report. 3. OPTIONAL — _total budget adjustment: Sprint 1+2 added ~19 new lazy chunks. Even if each is small (5-15 KB gzip), total JS may have grown. Should the `_total` cap of 500 KB be revisited in this PR, or kept for a separate audit?

### 19. Add missing testID props to unblock authored Detox e2e specs

- **id:** `detox-testid-props-source` - **kind:** code - **status:** plan-ready
- **Source:** `docs/audits/_runner-report.md`
- **Files:** `apps/mobile/app/(auth)/sign-up.tsx`, `apps/mobile/app/(auth)/forgot-password.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/core/SyncStatusIndicator.tsx`

**Summary.** After reading all 7 authored spec files and tracing every referenced testID back to source components, the only missing testIDs are 7 props across 2 auth screen files: `apps/mobile/app/(auth)/sign-up.tsx` (needs 4) and `apps/mobile/app/(auth)/forgot-password.tsx` (needs 3). Every nutrition, fizruk, finyk, routine, hub, and settings testID referenced by the other five suites is already wired. The `offline-sync` spec is explicitly written to fall back to `accessibilityLabel` and does not require new testIDs to become green. The two auth screens need only mechanical prop additions — no logic change, no new files.

**Steps:**

1. Read `apps/mobile/app/(auth)/sign-up.tsx` and add `testID` to the three Input elements (name, email, password) and the submit Button exactly as listed in the diff below.
2. Read `apps/mobile/app/(auth)/forgot-password.tsx` and add `testID` to the email Input, the submit Button, and the success-state root View exactly as listed in the diff below.
3. Push the branch; the Detox CI job (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) will exercise the seven specs. No local simulator run is needed to validate — CI is the gate per the task brief.
4. Optionally: add `testID` to the three SyncStatusIndicator state Views (`sync-status-offline`, `sync-status-idle`, `sync-status-syncing`) in `apps/mobile/src/core/SyncStatusIndicator.tsx` to make the offline-sync spec more robust (currently it falls back to `accessibilityLabel` and should still pass, but the spec comments explicitly ask for this upgrade).

**Risks.** Low. All changes are additive `testID` string props on existing JSX elements. They carry no runtime cost, no logic change, and no visual change. The only risk is a typo in a testID string — misspelling any of the 7 IDs causes the spec to stay red. The IDs are documented verbatim in the spec file headers so they can be cross-checked by inspection.

**Verification.** CI Detox job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs the full suite. After the diff is applied, all seven spec files (`auth-flows`, `nutrition-full`, `nutrition-water-barcode`, `fizruk-full`, `fizruk-measurements`, `deep-link`, `offline-sync`) should resolve every `waitForVisibleById` / `byId` call without a `No element found` error. A reviewer can also manually verify by searching each testID string from the spec files against the updated source and confirming a 1-to-1 match.

**Open questions.** None for the required 7 auth testIDs — they are unambiguous. One optional decision: whether to add the three SyncStatusIndicator testIDs in this PR or a follow-up. The offline-sync spec works without them (uses accessibilityLabel fallback), so this is purely a robustness choice.
